name: User Ratings
description: Allow users to rate and review movies, TV shows, and music. See what other users on your server think!
overview: Social rating system for Jellyfin - rate content and see ratings from other users on your server

versions.changelog: Removed unreliable scroll position restore — back-from-details now lands at top of User Ratings tab for stability. Removed vestigial userRatingsDirty sessionStorage flag (no remaining effect).

Fixed scroll position restore: was saving ratingsTab.scrollTop (always 0) instead of window.scrollY. Removed dead back-navigation code (restoreLastSection, popstate handler, lastVisibleSection tracking, unused variables). Merged duplicate hashchange listeners. Simplified MutationObserver options.

Fixed User Ratings tab breaking after navigation. #ratingsTab was getting trapped in hidden cached #indexPage copies (Jellyfin data-dom-cache). Now always recreates #ratingsTab inside the visible #indexPage on every tab click. Added scroll position save/restore for back-from-details.

Root cause fix for collapsed/ghost pages: #ratingsTab is now a real .tabContent.pageTabContent inside #indexPage (not a sibling overlay). Removed e.preventDefault() so Jellyfin's native tab system manages .is-active. No more stale tab state accumulating across navigation cycles.

Fixed collapsed/ghost pages after navigation (Home -> item -> Home -> Favorites left stale pages visible in the background). Plugin no longer toggles .hide/display on Jellyfin's own page elements; only manages its own #ratingsTab.

New: Average rating overlay on all Jellyfin cards (library browse, home, collections, search). Non-interactive badge shows star average and count. Performance: maintained server-side averages index, client cache with LRU eviction, single-flight batch fetching. Fixed: provider index going stale after 10-star migration.

Stable release. New: clickable Rate badge on unrated cards, half-star rating precision, version history table with install dates, configurable page size. Performance: server-side pagination for large libraries. UI: dashboard simplified to Rated Movies/Shows with sorting and pagination, Scheduled Sync section reorganized, Sync Watch History description clarified.

On browser refresh, Home tab is now shown (matching Jellyfin default for Favourites tab). Previously the tab highlight stayed on Home while User Ratings content was restored, causing inconsistent UI. Back-from-details restore is unaffected.

Fixed User Ratings tab highlight after browser refresh. Previously the tab content was restored but Home stayed marked active. Active highlight and content are now restored atomically when the tab button is injected.
Removed star icon from User Ratings tab to match native Jellyfin tab styling (other tabs have no icons).
Clarified Sync Watch History description: now states that all Plex watch history is synced, not just rated items.
Moved Enable Automatic Sync toggle to the top of the Scheduled Sync section, before the detailed sync options.
Moved Sync Now button to the end of the Scheduled Sync section, after Enable Automatic Sync.
Removed redundant Previous versions line from Migration section — Version History table is now the single source.

New: Version History table in plugin settings — scrollable, newest-first, with install dates. Current version highlighted in green. Old entries show Unknown date (legacy data had no timestamps). Data model upgraded transparently.
Restyled star icon on User Ratings tab: removed inline styles, added CSS rule for proper icon sizing (1.25em) and alignment. Star now renders like other Jellyfin Material Icons.
Fixed: Extra spacing between tab bar and content — removed redundant padding-top:4em wrapper (libraryPage class already provides native padding). Added: Outline star icon on User Ratings tab button.

Fixed persistent scroll shift: removed all scrollIntoView calls from section event handlers. Section containers are fixed DOM elements — only innerHTML changes on re-render, so controls stay in place naturally without forced scrolling.

Fixed: Sort direction toggle in unrated sections was broken (arrow never flipped, direction only changed one way). Fixed: Scroll shift after filter/sort changes pushed controls off-screen; now scrolls to section top with block:start.
Rate badge redesign: solid green gradient button look with border, shadow, and hover/active effects. Replaced flat transparent red label. Fully CSS-styled, no inline styles.

Configurable page size: RecentlyRatedItemsCount setting now controls items per page across all dashboard sections (was hardcoded 24). Unrated cards: removed separate RATE overlay gradient; existing Unrated badge is now a clickable Rate badge with star icon that opens rating popup. Cleaner UI, no duplicate overlay.

Dashboard simplified: Removed duplicate Recently Rated + All Rated sections. New Rated Movies and Rated Shows sections with sorting (Rating/Title/Recently Rated/Most Ratings), pagination, and direction toggle. Shows section has tab sub-filter (All/Shows/Episodes). Unrated cards now show always-visible RATE button instead of hover star overlay (better mobile UX). AllRatedItems API typeFilter accepts comma-separated values.
Fixed: Build warning from unused logger parameter in RatingResolver (leftover from v1.12.2.4 simplification)

Performance: Server-side pagination for All Rated Items (fetches 24 per page, not all at once); Batch library lookup replaces 386+ individual GetItemById calls; Removed provider-ID fallback from request path (stale items healed by scheduled task); Provider-ID index for O(1) FindByProviderIds; Health check interval 24h to 30min
New: Half-star precision in rating popup - click left/right half of any star to set .5 values; CSS gradient background-clip text fill; All display paths handle float values
Fixed: Popup stars fixated by hover (mouseleave handler moved to star row); Card not updating after rating (card reference captured before closeRatePopup clears it)
New: Dashboard hover-to-rate (Phase 1) - star overlay on card hover, popup modal with note, compact rating badge on rated cards

New: Plex watch history sync (optional flag to mark items as played); Consolidated config page (one-time import merged into Scheduled Sync); Progress bar resets to 0% for watch history phase. Also includes 10-star rating scale, DOM state sync, and various UI fixes
10-star rating scale with half-star precision; DOM state sync on back-navigation; Various UI fixes

Fixed: Plex Import conflict mode not passed to API. One-time import's On Conflict dropdown was cosmetic only; the API used the saved config value. Now the conflict mode is passed as a query parameter. Auto-sync has its own persisted SyncConflictMode setting.
Fixed: Plex Import UserName not set. Imported ratings showed Unknown User. PlexImportService now resolves username via IUserManager.GetUserById().

New: Global Exception Filter - unhandled API exceptions return JSON. Changed: Modern C# cleanup - file-scoped namespaces, primary constructors, record types. Typed Response DTOs - typed records for all API responses.

Fixed: Series Query Performance - from IsPlayed on Series (40s) to episode-based watched lookup (<50ms). Series Sort by Last Watched - uses episode's play date. Rating Oscillation - guards against re-keying. Rated Items Appearing as Unrated - server-side check. GUID Format Mismatch - all IDs use ToString(N).
Fixed: Back Button Returns to User Ratings Tab - uses history.replaceState to annotate entry. Switching to native tab clears the flag. No refetch on re-activation.

Fixed: Recently Rated / Oldest Rated Sort Broken - lastRatedTimestamp converted to epoch milliseconds. Changed: Sort Direction Toggle - replaced paired sort options with dropdown and direction button.
Fixed: Backup Files Table Width - changed from full-width to auto-fit.
Fixed: Plugin config page CSS not loading. Button text alignment, input styling, backup path width, table borders. New: Backup file management (download, restore, delete, upload).
Fixed: Script injection log spam - changed from Information to Debug level.
Provider ID resolution, self-healing ratings, database health checks, and automatic backups. Ratings matched by ItemId then provider IDs (IMDB, TMDB, TVDB). Fail-safe per-entry loading.
Unrated watched items browser, smart image fallbacks, type filters, independent section loading, and CI improvements.
Stable release
